horrid ass game

please play it

While it has its problems, I really enjoy this fighting game.
Pros
+ Compelling MVC style gameplay
+ Has rollback now
+ Beautiful animation
+ Android 21
+ The tide turning Dragon Wish mechanic
Mid:
~ I like the dramatic finishes, but having them be tied to stage is stupid
Cons:
- Too many buttons can do an auto-combo
- Cumbersome menu
- Please give us more stages
- Fairly aggressive DLC
- Worthless and antiquated DRM that makes it take a long time to load the game
- They stopped giving characters seasonal alternate colors at some point
- While I'm at it, I really wish there were more alternate costumes besides the one for Videl
- Boring story mode that serves no purpose other than unlocking a character for free
- Why does Vegito gotta be SSJBlue. Give the man SSJ2.

FighterZ is not only THE best Dragon Ball game but also THE best fighting game I ever played in my life, and I'd consider it my fav game of all time. 

Had so many great memories and think it suits all kinds of players, whether you are a casual, a competitive player, or just a Dragon Ball fan, the game is made for all. It has a fast paced style and it's really easy to get into while having satisfying depths to dive into if you're willing to. It also fixed an annoying common issue for me within Dragon Ball fighting games, which is having the whole roster feel very copy pasted with just different animations for basic attacks and different supers and ults. While this game offers a very humble number of characters compared to any other DB game, it offers the most unique and varied characters and playstyles ever in any DB game.

Also, not only does the game look so good visually and feel alive straight out of an anime episode or a manga panel, but the amount of detail and love put into this game is incredible. You can pause many of these characters' attacks and see how they can literally match some manga panels. They did not need to go that far, but Arc System is just that good of a studio.

The studio is so good they made their own original, non canon character that was made only for this game actually canon.

The cons:
- Not having crossplay, which in this day and age is a necessity for a fighting game, especially if on Steam since the bigger player base of fighting games is always on console, so that's bad news for a casual like me.
- The fact that there is some kind of content that is either locked for local play only (like 1v1) or locked for timed events only, like raid bosses
- Very boring gameplay for story mode; you always have to fight a bunch of clones until the next big main fight, and it just feels dragged (just boring, repetitive AI fights). It makes you not invested in whatever is going on in the story despite having a good story. Also, the cutscenes feel lazy and lack effort; it's mostly just the 3D models standing, so avoid this game if you're here for the story.

the game booted up. wouldn't accept inputs from PS5 Duelsense controller. 

After i enabled steam inputs.. i got to training mode and with in 3 mins of training mode the game crashed. tried rebooting the game.. froze my entire PC.

reset my PC and tried again thinking it was a fluke. crashed my PC again. reset again.

tried turning off steam inputs. Game didn't allow me to play it (no input detection from Duelsense. tried closing the game... crashed my PC

Third PC reset. Updated my nvidia drivers. Game crashed on boot of easy anti cheat. loaded up the game again. got into game, but no input from Duelsense, Tried to close, crashed my PC again. Reset my PC for the 4th time

Booted up the game, crashed on Easy Anti Cheat bootup. tried again and loaded the game while using PS4 Duelshock (with no steam input). Wouldn't let me play the game (not detecting inputs at all). Tried closing the game... crashed my PC again. this is now my 5th PC reset and like the 7th game crash on boot in a total play time of 30 mins.

i already requested a refund. because why is a game that literally DOESNT work on a steam sale at all.

this was a rebuy from me moving over to PC and I couldn't be more disappointed in this port of the game. 

This will stay a negative review until these problems are resolved because how in gods name are you releasing DLC for a game that cant even boot. This has to be breaking some kind of law.

Amazing game, the visuals are top tier definitively deserves a sequel or at LEAST some more DLC characters. The game also goes on sale quite often, so its a great buy if you are at least a little interested.

Really enjoyed this game. Very exiting and filled with action, as figuring out new moves and combos mid battle makes it feel much more exiting and engaging while also challenging. Definitely recommend to anyone who loves Dragon Ball and just action in general.

Ive owned this game since day one. This game used to be pretty fun. However most DLC characters are overtuned and make online ranked terrible. The story for this game is pretty ♥♥♥♥ tier at best and the repetitiveness leaves it a chore to finish, and with the introduction of ss4 daima goku he can nearly one shot a character with a spammy down smash juggle. I loved this game near release. But unless you plan to play casual or with friends. I cant recommend it anymore. The meta for this game sets up 100% kills,  the reflect mechanic basically got debuffed from combos allowing for you to cross the entire screen with a basic punch combo and it rewards you playing only Op characters (Fusions, UI, Ect..) call me a hater or whatever but when a ssg Blue can shoot 4 big bangs in a combo set or Super broly it makes for a really dimishing desire to play. Most dlc can either ignore attacks, get two screen vanishes or even just have setups thats just unfair. Once you hit 100,000 bp in ranked its all downhill. Be warned.
The developers of this game won't let you play online unless you're on windows. Steam decks cannot play online matchmaking, and Linux users cannot play online matchmaking. Absolute clownery.
looks like so much fun but like every other game ppl min/maxxed this game to ♥♥♥♥ and now if u dont get the first hit your basically done for nn get juggled the whole round lol

Played this game on the Switch a long time ago, noticed that it had a discount on Steam alongside some DLC and decided to play it again. I play casually, so the competitive side of the online mode isn't for me, but I digress.

It's no fun without friends and the story mode is mid at best (my opinion). But nonetheless it is still a good, flashy fighting game that doesn't involve complex inputs to attack, thus making it quite easy to learn yet hard to master. Just tag a friend along so that there is value in the gameplay.

The dramatic finishes are also cool. I kept high setting with a resolution of 1080p for the best playing experience in accordance to my laptop specs.

I've played fighters in the arcade and at home since probably 89, and started with Street Fighter 1. Over the years I started pulling away from Capcom and Namco just a little more due to all of the Arc System Works amazing games. They do the art up right, and the action is ALWAYS fluid. Blazblue, Guilty Gear, etc. These guys have worked very hard to become the current king of fighters. I also grew up watching Dragon Ball, and Dragon Ball Z via imports and Toonami. This is a pairing made in heaven. If you were to show this to my 13 year old self I would absolutely freak out. Everything is here, and damn is it fast. The supers, characters, and levels look incredible. My only gripe would be how they do the DLC for this stuff anymore. My recommendation is to wait for the bundle package to go on sale, and get all characters in one shot. Do NOT sleep on this one! It's been out for awhile now, and IMO it's still the best 2-D fighter out there. Amazing what they did here.

arcsys make dbfz 2 and my life is yours!!!!!!!!! In all seriousness  if you aren't into fighters I think this is genuinely the best way to get into them. Sure is there years of tech skill and all the characters pretty much figured out? Absolutely! However this is  a title you can pick up and play while also mastering the long enjoyed characters we've all come to love from the Dragon Ball franchise.  Trust me, pick your favorite character and beat your friends up.
Do I recommend this game? No, stay away from it, you're gonna be stuck on one rank wondering why so many people mash and play so passively.
